assertions.text: Identifies that CurrentMemoryContext inside expr eval is the per-tuple ExprContext and gets reset between tuple cycles
Notes that the per-tuple context is reset at the START of each tuple cycle (not the end), per README:308-368
Recommends multi_call_memory_ctx from SRF_FIRSTCALL_INIT/FuncCallContext for value-per-call SRFs
Recommends rsinfo->econtext->ecxt_per_query_memory for Materialize-mode tuplestore allocation
Mentions datumCopy / pstrdup as the way to move pass-by-reference data across the boundary
Cites a specific knowledge/idioms or source file (file:line or named file) for the per-tuple lifetime claim
Shows the MemoryContextSwitchTo save/restore idiom with oldcxt
States that palloc directly into CacheMemoryContext leaks for backend lifetime
Recommends creating a CHILD context of CacheMemoryContext and storing it on the cache entry
Recommends ALLOCSET_SMALL_SIZES preset for per-relation cache contexts
Notes that AllocSetContextCreate name must be a string literal (StaticAssertExpr) and to use MemoryContextSetIdentifier for dynamic names
Names a concrete relcache.c precedent field (rd_rulescxt, rd_indexcxt, or rd_smgrcxt)
Mentions that MemoryContextDelete is recursive so a single delete frees the entire subtree
Cites SKILL.md or knowledge/idioms/memory-contexts.md (not just source) for the rule

evals.question_short: 2 GB alloc and huge allocation API

assertions.text: Identifies MaxAllocSize = 1 GB - 1 as the cap that triggered the error
Recommends palloc_extended(sz, MCXT_ALLOC_HUGE) or MemoryContextAllocHuge as the correct API
Mentions repalloc_huge for resizing huge chunks
States MaxAllocHugeSize = SIZE_MAX/2 as the huge cap
Confirms OOM still throws ereport(ERROR) unless MCXT_ALLOC_NO_OOM is set
Notes that Bump does NOT support pfree/repalloc and is therefore unsuitable for a growable buffer
Mentions AggCheckCallContext / aggcontext as the correct context for aggregate transition state
Cites memutils.h:40-49 or knowledge file with file:line for MaxAllocHugeSize
